sources.program_name: Georgia Job Tax Credit and Quality Jobs Tax Credit
sources.evidence_summary: Georgia's flagship job-related tax credits evaluate job creation, location, industry, wage levels, and maintenance of qualifying jobs.
sources.source_note: Official Georgia Department of Revenue tax-credit summary page.
diligence_questions: Which county tier or special zone applies to the final site?
Do the proposed jobs fall within a qualifying industry sector?
Will wage levels support Quality Jobs Tax Credit review?

sources.evidence_summary: Georgia Quick Start provides customized workforce training for qualified new and expanding companies and is positioned as an in-kind discretionary incentive.
sources.source_note: Official Georgia Quick Start program page.
diligence_questions: What roles, skills, and hiring waves must the training plan cover?
When must training start relative to construction and commissioning?
Which technical college or Quick Start team would support delivery?

sources.program_name: Georgia discretionary project finance and local support
sources.evidence_summary: Georgia economic-development finance resources can support private job creation and expansion projects, often coordinated with state and local development partners.
sources.source_note: Official Georgia Department of Community Affairs REBA program page.
diligence_questions: Is the project competitive with another state or local jurisdiction?
Which public infrastructure or site-readiness gap needs support?
What local match or development authority participation is available?

sources.program_name: North Carolina Job Development Investment Grant
sources.evidence_summary: JDIG is a performance-based discretionary incentive for competitive job-creation and investment projects in North Carolina.
sources.source_note: Official North Carolina Department of Commerce JDIG program page.
diligence_questions: Can the company document a competitive location decision?
How do proposed wages compare with the county average wage?
What job and investment targets can the company commit to annually?

sources.evidence_summary: OneNC is a discretionary cash-grant program used to respond quickly to competitive job-creation projects and requires local participation.
sources.source_note: Official North Carolina Department of Commerce One North Carolina Fund page.
diligence_questions: What local match can the community provide?
Which job, wage, and investment commitments are realistic for the performance agreement?
How urgent is the competitive location decision?

sources.evidence_summary: North Carolina's Customized Training Program provides training and support services through the state's community college network for new, expanding, and existing businesses.
sources.source_note: Official North Carolina Department of Commerce customized training page.
diligence_questions: Which community college would deliver the training plan?
What technical skills and productivity outcomes should training address?
What hiring schedule should the training support?

sources.program_name: North Carolina Job Ready Workforce Investment Grants
sources.evidence_summary: Job Ready Grants support recruitment, talent development, and work-based learning activities tied to economic development projects in North Carolina.
sources.source_note: Official North Carolina Department of Commerce Job Ready Grants page.
diligence_questions: Which local workforce board would sponsor or coordinate the application?
What recruiting and talent pipeline gaps need funding?
How many new hires will be tied directly to the project?

sources.evidence_summary: South Carolina's Job Development Credit reimburses approved companies from new employee withholding for eligible capital expenditures tied to new full-time jobs.
sources.source_note: Official South Carolina Department of Commerce state discretionary incentives page.
diligence_questions: Does the project create at least 10 new full-time jobs or meet a special facility threshold?
Will employees receive qualifying health benefits?
How do wages compare with the county average wage?

sources.program_name: South Carolina Set-Aside, Closing Fund, and Rural Infrastructure Fund
sources.evidence_summary: South Carolina discretionary grant funds can support road, water, sewer, infrastructure, site improvements, and high-impact economic development projects.
sources.source_note: Official South Carolina Department of Commerce state discretionary incentives page.
diligence_questions: Which local government or public entity would sponsor infrastructure support?
What road, water, sewer, or site-improvement need is tied to the project?
Is the site in a rural county tier or a high-impact recruitment context?

sources.evidence_summary: readySC provides recruiting and training assistance through the South Carolina Technical College System for expanding or relocating companies.
sources.source_note: Official South Carolina Department of Commerce workforce incentives page.
diligence_questions: Which roles and technical skills need a custom curriculum?
Which technical college would support delivery?
When must recruiting and training begin relative to commissioning?

sources.evidence_summary: Tennessee FastTrack Job Training Assistance helps offset costs for training new employees, upgraded employees, and retained employees tied to expansion or relocation activity.
sources.source_note: Official Transparent Tennessee FastTrack program reporting and metrics pages.
diligence_questions: What new-hire or upgraded-position training costs need support?
What hiring schedule and skill profile can be documented?
Which FastTrack reporting obligations would apply?

sources.evidence_summary: FastTrack Infrastructure Development is one of Tennessee's grant programs used to support public infrastructure needs associated with expansion or relocation projects.
sources.source_note: Official Transparent Tennessee FastTrack program reporting and metrics pages.
diligence_questions: What public infrastructure scope is required for the project?
Which public sponsor would own or deliver the improvements?
How are job and capex commitments tied to the infrastructure need?

sources.evidence_summary: Tennessee's FastTrack Economic Development grants can offset expansion and relocation costs for projects with job-creation commitments.
sources.source_note: Official Transparent Tennessee FastTrack program reporting and metrics pages.
diligence_questions: What company costs remain influenceable by a grant?
Which jobs and capital investment commitments can be contracted?
Does the project have a competitive location decision or retention risk?

sources.evidence_summary: The Texas Enterprise Fund awards deal-closing cash grants for competitive projects with significant capital investment and new employment where a Texas site competes with an out-of-state option.
sources.source_note: Official Texas Economic Development & Tourism Office Texas Enterprise Fund page.
diligence_questions: Is one Texas site competing with a viable out-of-state option before a location decision?
Does job creation exceed the urban or rural threshold?
Will average wages meet or exceed the county average wage?

sources.evidence_summary: The Texas Skills Development Fund supports customized training through eligible public training partners for new workers or upgraded skills for existing workers.
sources.source_note: Official Texas Workforce Commission Skills Development Fund page.
diligence_questions: Which eligible college, workforce board, or TEEX partner would apply?
Are trainees full-time W-2 employees?
What training curriculum and wage outcomes can be documented?

sources.evidence_summary: The Texas Enterprise Zone Program is a state sales and use tax refund program requiring community nomination, investment/job thresholds, and qualifying employee criteria.
sources.source_note: Official Texas Economic Development & Tourism Office Enterprise Zone Program page.
diligence_questions: Will the local community nominate the company?
Which capital investment and job creation or retention bracket applies?
Can qualifying employee criteria be documented?

sources.evidence_summary: Ohio's Job Creation Tax Credit is a refundable performance-based credit tied to created payroll and requires approval by the Ohio Tax Credit Authority.
sources.source_note: Official JobsOhio incentives eligibility and requirements page.
diligence_questions: Can the company create at least 10 jobs within three years?
Will new payroll meet current program thresholds?
What Tax Credit Authority approval timing applies?

sources.evidence_summary: JobsOhio Workforce Grant support may consider job creation, employee training, operational efficiency, production expansion, and job retention.
sources.source_note: Official JobsOhio incentives eligibility and requirements page.
diligence_questions: What training expenses are tied to job creation or production expansion?
Which jobs are new, retained, or upskilled?
What training period and documentation requirements apply?

sources.evidence_summary: JobsOhio revitalization assistance can support site redevelopment needs where development and job creation are likely.
sources.source_note: Official JobsOhio incentives eligibility and requirements page.
diligence_questions: Is there a brownfield, vacant, or underutilized site condition?
What redevelopment cost is blocking the project?
What job creation or reuse outcome supports the public case?

sources.program_name: Indiana Economic Development for a Growing Economy Tax Credit
sources.evidence_summary: Indiana EDGE provides a refundable payroll tax credit to support job creation, capital investment, and improved standards of living for Indiana residents.
sources.source_note: Official Indiana Economic Development Corporation EDGE page.
diligence_questions: What hiring ramp and withholding increase can be committed?
What payroll and job-retention compliance reporting would apply?
Does the project require IEDC approval before commitments are made?

sources.evidence_summary: Indiana's Skills Enhancement Fund reimburses a portion of eligible training costs tied to new capital investment and skill upgrades.
sources.source_note: Official Indiana Economic Development Corporation SEF page.
diligence_questions: Will training lead to post-secondary credentials, recognized industry credentials, or specialized company training?
Which employee groups and wage increases are tied to the project?
What costs fall within the grant agreement dates?

sources.program_name: Indiana Hoosier Business Investment Tax Credit
sources.evidence_summary: Indiana's Hoosier Business Investment Tax Credit supports capital investment and job creation through non-refundable corporate income tax credits based on eligible capital investment.
sources.source_note: Official Indiana Economic Development Corporation HBI page.
diligence_questions: What eligible capital investment can be phased in during the certification period?
What job creation or standard-of-living impact supports the project?
What approval must occur before investment commitments?

sources.evidence_summary: Alabama Jobs Act incentives include a discretionary jobs credit based on eligible employee wages, with lower thresholds for targeted, jumpstart, technology, and specified industry projects.
sources.source_note: Official Made in Alabama Jobs Act incentive summary.
diligence_questions: Is the site in a targeted or jumpstart county?
Does the activity qualify under the Jobs Act business activity list?
What net new full-time job threshold applies to this project type?

sources.evidence_summary: Alabama's Investment Credit can provide a discretionary credit based on qualified capital investment for approved projects that create jobs in Alabama.
sources.source_note: Official Alabama Department of Revenue Investment Credit page.
diligence_questions: Has the Alabama Department of Commerce approved the project path?
Which county/project threshold applies?
What qualified capital investment is eligible under the project agreement?

sources.evidence_summary: Kentucky Business Investment provides income tax credits and wage assessments to eligible new and expanding companies that locate or expand operations in Kentucky.
sources.source_note: Official Kentucky Cabinet for Economic Development financial incentives page.
diligence_questions: Does the project activity fall into a qualifying Kentucky industry category?
What jobs, wages, benefits, and eligible costs can be documented?
Is the county eligible for enhanced incentives?

sources.evidence_summary: BSSC provides Grant-in-Aid reimbursements and Skills Training Investment Credits for employer-driven skills-upgrade training at qualifying Kentucky businesses.
sources.source_note: Official Kentucky Cabinet for Economic Development BSSC page.
diligence_questions: What occupational or skills-upgrade training would be delivered?
Does the business activity qualify for BSSC incentives?
Which application deadline or board meeting applies?

sources.evidence_summary: EDA Public Works supports public infrastructure that helps communities attract industry, encourage business expansion, and create or retain long-term jobs.
sources.source_note: Official Grants.gov listing for EDA Public Works and Economic Adjustment Assistance programs.
diligence_questions: Is there a public infrastructure gap connected to the project?
Is an eligible public or nonprofit applicant available?
Does the project align with a regional economic development strategy?

sources.evidence_summary: EDA Economic Adjustment Assistance can support strategy and implementation projects, including infrastructure improvements and workforce-related activities, in regions facing economic change.
sources.source_note: Official Grants.gov listing for EDA Public Works and Economic Adjustment Assistance programs.
diligence_questions: What adverse economic change or regional need supports the public case?
Is the proposed activity included in a CEDS or equivalent strategy?
Who is the eligible applicant and match provider?
